(4) where assignment of debt / recovery proceedings is initiated - the additional
provisions may be reversed upon completion of the assignment of debt /
recovery.
enriched_requirements.object: of the assignment of debt / recovery

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of this Chapter shall be applicable to all restructurings, including those
undertaken under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016.
enriched_requirements.object: restructurings, including those undertaken under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An account classified as 'standard' shall be immediately downgraded as non-
performing assets, i.e., 'sub-standard' to begin with, following a restructuring by an
AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: non- performing assets, i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An account classified as non-performing asset, upon restructuring by an AIFI, shall
continue to have the same asset classification as prior to restructuring.
enriched_requirements.object: same asset classification as prior to restructuring

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In both above cases, the asset classification shall continue to be governed by the
ageing criteria as per extant asset classification norms contained in the Reserve
Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Income Recognition, Asset
Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: by the ageing criteria as per extant asset classification norms contained in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial In

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any additional finance approved under the resolution plan (including any resolution
plan approved by the Adjudicating Authority under Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code,
2016) may be treated by an AIFI as standard asset during the monitoring period
under the approved resolution plan, provided the account demonstrates satisfactory
performance during the monitoring period.
enriched_requirements.object: AIFI as standard asset during the monitoring period under the approved resolution plan

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If the restructured asset fails to perform satisfactorily during the monitoring period
or does not qualify for upgradation at the end of the monitoring period, the additional
finance shall be placed in the same asset classification category as the restructured
debt by an AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: same asset classification category as the restructured debt by an AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any interim finance [as defined in section 5 (15) of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy
Code, 2016] extended by the specified lenders to borrowers undergoing insolvency
<image>
proceedings under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 may be treated as
standard asset during the insolvency resolution process period as defined in the
Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016.
enriched_requirements.object: asset during the insolvency resolution process period as defined in the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Standard accounts classified as non-performing and non-performing accounts
retained in the same category on restructuring by an AIFI may be upgraded only
when all the outstanding loan / credit facilities in the account demonstrate
'satisfactory performance' with respect to all the specified lenders during the
monitoring period.
enriched_requirements.object: all the outstanding loan / credit facilities in the account demonstrate 'satisfactory performance' with respect to all the speci

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the account cannot be upgraded before one year from the
commencement of the first payment of interest or principal (whichever is later) on
the credit facility with longest period of moratorium under the terms of RP.
enriched_requirements.object: upgraded before one year from the commencement of the first payment of interest or principal (whichever is later) on the credit

conditions:
where the aggregate
exposure of specified lenders is ₹100 crore and above at the time of implementation
of resolution plan
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the requirement in paragraph 55, for accounts where the aggregate
exposure of specified lenders is ₹100 crore and above at the time of implementation
of resolution plan, to qualify for an upgrade, in addition to demonstration of
satisfactory performance, the credit facilities of the borrower shall also be rated as
investment grade (BBB- or better), at the time of upgrade, by credit rating agencies
accredited by the Reserve Bank for the purpose of bank loan ratings.
enriched_requirements.object: investment grade (BBB- or better), at the time of upgrade, by credit rating agencies accredited by the Reserve Bank for the purpose o

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that while accounts with aggregate exposure of ₹500 crore and above shall
require two ratings, those below ₹500 crore shall require one rating.
enriched_requirements.object: below ₹500 crore shall require one rating

conditions:
if the ratings are obtained from more than the required number
of credit rating agencies
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that if the ratings are obtained from more than the required number
of credit rating agencies, all such ratings shall be investment grade for the account
to qualify for an upgrade.
enriched_requirements.object: the account to qualify for an upgrade

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: These ratings referred to in the second proviso shall be the normal
ratings provided by the credit rating agencies and not Independent Credit Evaluation
specified in paragraph 29.
enriched_requirements.object: provided by the credit rating agencies and not Independent Credit Evaluation specified in paragraph 29

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If a borrower fails to demonstrate satisfactory performance with respect to all the
specified lenders during the monitoring period, asset classification upgrade shall be
subject to implementation of a fresh restructuring / change in ownership under these
Directions or under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016.
enriched_requirements.object: of a fresh restructuring / change in ownership under these Directions or under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An account that has been restructured shall attract provisioning as per the asset
classification category as laid out in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions,
2025.
enriched_requirements.object: the asset classification category as laid out in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Income Reco

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall make additional specific provision of 15 per cent for an account
referred to in paragraphs 57 and 58 at the end of the Review Period.
enriched_requirements.object: of 15 per cent for an account referred to in paragraphs 57 and 58 at the end of the Review Period

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Specific provisions held on restructured assets by an AIFI as per paragraph 59
above, may be reversed when the accounts are upgraded to standard category.
enriched_requirements.object: accounts are upgraded to standard category

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Additional specific provisions as required under paragraph 60, along with other
additional specific provisions, may be reversed as per the norms laid down in
paragraph 47 by an AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: the norms laid down in paragraph 47 by an AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
During the insolvency resolution process period, provisioning for the interim finance
shall be governed by the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions -
Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 20

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Subsequently, upon approval of the resolution plan by the Adjudicating Authority,
treatment of such interim finance shall be as per the norms applicable to additional
finance, as per paragraphs 52 and 53.
enriched_requirements.object: norms applicable to additional finance, as per paragraphs 52 and 53

conditions: where a final resolution plan
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of accounts of borrowers where a final resolution plan, as approved by
the Committee of Creditors, has been submitted by the Resolution Professional for
approval of the Adjudicating Authority (in terms of section 30(6) of the Insolvency
and Bankruptcy Code, 2016), an AIFI may keep the provisions held as on the date
of such submission of RP frozen for a period of six months from the date of
submission of the plan or up to 90 days from the date of approval of the resolution
plan by the Adjudicating Authority in terms of section 31(1) of the Insolvency and
Bankruptcy Code, 2016, whichever is earlier.
enriched_requirements.object: as on the date of such submission of RP frozen for a period of six months from the date of submission of the plan or up to 90

conditions:
where the provisioning held is lower than the expected
required provisioning
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that in cases where the provisioning held is lower than the expected
required provisioning, an AIFI shall make additional provisioning to the extent of the
shortfall.
enriched_requirements.object: the extent of the shortfall

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The facility of freezing the quantum of the provision as per paragraph 65 shall be
available only in cases where the provisioning held by an AIFI as on the date of
submission of the plan for approval of the Adjudicating Authority is more than the
expected provisioning required to be held in the normal course upon implementation
of the approved resolution plan, taking into account the contours of the resolution
plan approved by Committee of Creditors / Adjudicating Authority, as the case may
be, and extant prudential norms.
enriched_requirements.object: cases where the provisioning held by an AIFI as on the date of submission of the plan for approval of the Adjudicating Authority

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding paragraph 66, an AIFI shall not reverse the excess provisions held
as on the date of submission of the resolution plan for approval of the Adjudicating
Authority at this stage.
enriched_requirements.object: held as on the date of submission of the resolution plan for approval of the Adjudicating Authority at this stage

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Subsequent to the lapse of the period specified in paragraph 65, provisioning shall
be as per the requirements in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions,
2025.
enriched_requirements.object: requirements in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning)

conditions: if the Adjudicating Authority rejects the resolution plan thus submitted
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The facility of freezing of provisions as allowed in paragraph 65 shall lapse
immediately if the Adjudicating Authority rejects the resolution plan thus submitted,
and asset classification in respect of such borrower shall continue be governed by
the extant asset classification norms.
enriched_requirements.object: Adjudicating Authority rejects the resolution plan thus submitted, and asset classification in respect of such borrower shall

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Interest income in respect of restructured accounts classified as 'standard assets'
may be recognized on accrual basis and that in respect of the restructured accounts
classified as 'non-performing assets' shall be recognised on cash basis.
enriched_requirements.object: basis

conditions:
where the pre-restructuring facilities
were classified as non-performing assets
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of additional finance in accounts where the pre-restructuring facilities
were classified as non-performing assets, the interest income shall be recognised
only on cash basis by an AIFI except when the restructuring is accompanied by a
change in ownership.
enriched_requirements.object: cash basis by an AIFI except when the restructuring is accompanied by a change in ownership

conditions: In case of change in ownership of the borrowing entities
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case of change in ownership of the borrowing entities, credit facilities of the
concerned borrowing entities may be continued / upgraded as 'standard' by an AIFI
after the change in ownership is implemented, either under the Insolvency and
Bankruptcy Code, 2016 or under these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: as 'standard' by an AIFI after the change in ownership is implemented, either under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 o

conditions: If the change in ownership is implemented under these Directions
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If the change in ownership is implemented under these Directions, then the
classification as 'standard' shall be subject to the following conditions:
(1) The AIFI shall conduct necessary due diligence in this regard and clearly
establish that the acquirer is not a person disqualified in terms of Section
29A of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016.
enriched_requirements.object: following conditions: (1) The AIFI shall conduct necessary due diligence in this regard and clearly establish that the acquirer is n

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The AIFI shall clearly establish that the 'new promoter' is not a person /
entity / subsidiary / associate etc.
enriched_requirements.object: 'new promoter' is not a person / entity / subsidiary / associate etc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(3) The new promoter shall have acquired at least 26 per cent of the paid-up
equity capital as well as voting rights of the borrower entity and shall be the
single largest shareholder of the borrower entity.
enriched_requirements.object: 26 per cent of the paid-up equity capital as well as voting rights of the borrower entity and shall be the single largest share

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The new promoter shall be in 'control' of the borrower entity as per the
definition of 'control' in the Companies Act, 2013 / regulations issued by the
Securities and Exchange Board of India / any other applicable regulations /
accounting standards, as the case may be.
enriched_requirements.object: the borrower entity as per the definition of 'control' in the Companies Act, 2013 / regulations issued by the Securities and Exchan

conditions:
If the account fails to perform satisfactorily at any point of time during the monitoring
period
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If the account fails to perform satisfactorily at any point of time during the monitoring
period, it shall trigger a fresh Review Period.
enriched_requirements.object: Period

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The quantum of provisions held (excluding additional provisions) by the AIFI against
the said account as on the date of change in ownership of the borrowing entities
may be reversed only after the end of monitoring period subject to satisfactory
performance during the same.
enriched_requirements.object: the end of monitoring period subject to satisfactory performance during the same

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case the promoters' shares have been pledged with an AIFI as collateral during
the lock-in period mandated under the SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure
Requirements) Regulations, 2018, and the pledge is invoked during the lock-in
period by the AIFI consequent to default by the company, such shares may be
transferred to the AIFI but shall continue to be under lock-in in the hands of the AIFI
for the remaining lock-in period.
enriched_requirements.object: lock-in in the hands of the AIFI for the remaining lock-in period

conditions:
if all the following conditions are met:
(1) the seller of the assets is in financial difficulty
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A sale and leaseback transaction of the assets of a borrower or other transactions
of similar nature shall be treated as an event of restructuring for the purpose of asset
classification and provisioning in the books of a bank with regard to the residual debt
of the seller as well as the debt of the buyer if all the following conditions are met:
(1) the seller of the assets is in financial difficulty;
enriched_requirements.object: event of restructuring for the purpose of asset classification and provisioning in the books of a bank with regard to the residual de

conditions:
where permitted) from the Indian banking system in the
form of Guarantees / Standby Letters of Credit / Letters of Comfort
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
or
(2) with the support (where permitted) from the Indian banking system in the
form of Guarantees / Standby Letters of Credit / Letters of Comfort, etc.,
such events shall be treated as 'restructuring' by an AIFI if the borrower concerned
is under financial difficulty.
enriched_requirements.object: by an AIFI if the borrower concerned is under financial difficulty

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Borrowers who have committed frauds/ malfeasance/ wilful default as well as any
entity with which a wilful defaulter is associated shall remain ineligible for
restructuring.

However, in cases where the existing promoters are replaced by new promoters
satisfying the conditions specified at sub-paragraphs 73(1) to 73(4), and the borrower
company is totally delinked from such erstwhile promoters / management, an AIFI
may take a view on restructuring such accounts based on their viability, without
prejudice to the continuance of criminal action against the erstwhile promoters /
management.
enriched_requirements.object: restructuring such accounts based on their viability, without prejudice to the continuance of criminal action against the erstwhile pro

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A wilful defaulter or any entity with which a wilful defaulter is associated shall be
eligible for restructuring subsequent to removal of the name of wilful defaulter from
the List of Wilful Defaulters, subject to penal measures applicable to borrowers
classified as wilful defaulter in terms of the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Treatment of Wilful Defaulters and Large Defaulters) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: subsequent to removal of the name of wilful defaulter from the List of Wilful Defaulters

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The objective of compromise settlements shall be to maximise the possible recovery
from a distressed borrower at minimum expense, in the best interest of the AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: possible recovery from a distressed borrower at minimum expense, in the best interest of the AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The compromise settlements and technical write-offs shall be without prejudice to
any mutually agreed contractual provisions between an AIFI and a borrower relating
to future contingent realizations or recovery by the AIFI, subject to such claims not
being recognised in any manner on the balance sheet of the AIFI at the time of the
settlement or subsequently till actual realization of such receivables.
enriched_requirements.object: any mutually agreed contractual provisions between an AIFI and a borrower relating to future contingent realizations or recovery by the AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that any such claims recognised on the balance sheet of the AIFI shall
render the arrangement to be treated as restructuring.
enriched_requirements.object: be treated as restructuring

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding paragraph 85, compromise settlements where the time for payment
of the agreed settlement amount exceeds three months shall be treated as
restructuring.

Any arrangement involving part settlement with the borrower shall also fall under the
definition of restructuring, and shall be governed by the provisions applicable
thereto.
enriched_requirements.object: definition of restructuring, and shall be governed by the provisions applicable thereto

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However,
such technical write-offs do not entail any waiver of claims against the borrower and
thus the AIFI's right to recovery shall not undermined in any manner.

conditions: In case of partial technical write-offs
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case of partial technical write-offs, the prudential requirements in respect of
residual exposure, including provisioning and asset classification, shall be with
reference to the original exposure.
enriched_requirements.object: the original exposure

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the amount of provision including the amount representing partial
technical write-off shall meet the extant provisioning requirements, as computed on
the gross value of the asset.
enriched_requirements.object: requirements, as computed on the gross value of the asset

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
There shall be a reporting mechanism to the next higher authority, at least on a
quarterly basis, with respect to compromise settlements and technical write offs
approved by a particular authority.
enriched_requirements.object: to the next higher authority, at least on a quarterly basis, with respect to compromise settlements and technical write offs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that compromise settlements and technical write-offs approved by the MD
& CEO / Board Level Committee shall be reported to the Board.
enriched_requirements.object: Board

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall mandate a suitable reporting format so as to ensure adequate
coverage of the following aspects at the minimum:
<image>
(1) trend in number of accounts and amounts subjected to compromise
settlement and/or technical write-off (q-o-q and y-o-y);
enriched_requirements.object: format so as to ensure adequate coverage of the following aspects at the minimum: <image> (1) trend in number of accounts

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of borrowers subject to compromise settlements, there shall be a cooling
period as determined by the respective Board approved policies before the AIFI can
assume fresh exposures to such borrowers.
enriched_requirements.object: as determined by the respective Board approved policies before the AIFI can assume fresh exposures to such borrowers

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the cooling period in respect of exposures other than farm credit
exposures shall be subject to a floor of 12 months with an AIFI being free to stipulate
higher cooling periods in terms of their Board approved policies.
enriched_requirements.object: floor of 12 months with an AIFI being free to stipulate higher cooling periods in terms of their Board approved policies

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that the cooling period for farm credit exposures shall be
determined by an AIFI as per their respective Board approved policies.
enriched_requirements.object: AIFI as per their respective Board approved policies

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: Farm credit for the above purpose shall refer to credit extended to
agricultural activities as listed in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions,
2025.
enriched_requirements.object: to agricultural activities as listed in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Income Recognition, Ass

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The cooling period to be adopted in respect of exposures subjected to technical
write-offs shall be as per the Board approved policies of an AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: Board approved policies of an AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI may undertake compromise settlements or technical write-offs in respect of
accounts categorised as wilful defaulters or fraud without prejudice to the criminal
proceeding underway against such borrowers.
enriched_requirements.object: technical write-offs in respect of accounts categorised as wilful defaulters or fraud without prejudice to the cri

conditions:
where an AIFI enter into compromise settlement with such
borrowers
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The penal measures applicable to borrowers classified as fraud or wilful defaulter in
terms of the Reserve Bank of India (Fraud Risk Management in Commercial Banks
<image>
(including Regional Rural Banks) and All India Financial Institutions) Directions,
2024 and the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Treatment of
Wilful Defaulters and Large Defaulters) Directions, 2025, respectively, shall continue
to be applicable in cases where an AIFI enter into compromise settlement with such
borrowers, and the cooling periods specified in paragraphs 92 and 93, in respect of
such borrowers, shall be without prejudice to such penal measures.
enriched_requirements.object: in cases where an AIFI enter into compromise settlement with such borrowers, and the cooling periods specified in paragraphs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
As long as larger policy concerns are suitably addressed and the costs of malafide
actions are made to be borne by the perpetrators, early recoveries by an AIFI should
be a preferred option, subject to safeguards.

The compromise settlements with the borrowers under these Directions shall be
without prejudice to the provisions of any other statute in force.
enriched_requirements.object: the provisions of any other statute in force

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Wherever an AIFI had commenced recovery proceedings under a judicial forum and
the same is pending before such judicial forum, any settlement arrived at with the
borrower shall be subject to obtaining a consent decree from the concerned judicial
authorities.
enriched_requirements.object: a consent decree from the concerned judicial authorities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the requirements contained in paragraphs 83 to 97, the following shall
apply in cases of compromise settlements undertaken by an AIFI through Lok
Adalats:
(1) The monetary ceiling of the cases to be referred to the Lok Adalats,
organised by Civil Courts shall be ₹20 lakh.
enriched_requirements.object: compromise settlements undertaken by an AIFI through Lok Adalats: (1) The monetary ceiling of the cases to be referred to the Lok Ad

conditions: where financial closure has been achieved as on October 1
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The instructions contained in paragraphs 101 to 119 shall not apply to projects
where financial closure has been achieved as on October 1, 2025, for which the
prudential guidelines on project finance prevailing before October 1, 2025, which
otherwise shall be treated as repealed, shall apply.
enriched_requirements.object: financial closure has been achieved as on October 1, 2025, for which the prudential guidelines on project finance prevailing b

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the instructions in paragraph 99, any resolution of a fresh credit
event and/or change in material terms and conditions in the loan contract in such
projects, on or after October 1, 2025, shall be as per the guidelines contained in
paragraphs 101 to 119.
enriched_requirements.object: guidelines contained in paragraphs 101 to 119

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall monitor the performance of the project and any buildup of stress on an
ongoing basis and shall be expected to initiate a resolution plan well in advance.
enriched_requirements.object: the project and any buildup of stress on an ongoing basis and shall be expected to initiate a resolution plan well in advan

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Occurrence of a credit event with any lender during the construction phase, shall
trigger a collective resolution in terms of Chapter III of these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: in terms of Chapter III of these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The reference to 'default' in Chapter III of these Directions shall be read
as 'credit event' for the purpose of project finance accounts, unless specified
otherwise.
enriched_requirements.object: event' for the purpose of project finance accounts

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any such credit event with an AIFI shall be reported to the Central Repository of
Information on Large Credit (CRILC) by the AIFI in the prescribed weekly as well as
the CRILC-Main report in compliance with paragraphs 15 and 16.
enriched_requirements.object: Central Repository of Information on Large Credit (CRILC) by the AIFI in the prescribed weekly as well as the CRILC-Main report in

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI, which is part of a consortium / multiple lending arrangement shall also
report occurrence of such credit event to all other members of the consortium /
multiple lending arrangement.
enriched_requirements.object: such credit event to all other members of the consortium / multiple lending arrangement

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The instructions on CRILC reporting as per paragraph 103 shall be issued in due
course.
enriched_requirements.object: course

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall undertake a prima facie review of the borrower account during the
Review Period.
enriched_requirements.object: review of the borrower account during the Review Period

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The conduct of an AIFI during the Review Period, including signing of Inter Creditor
Agreement (ICA), and the decision to implement a resolution plan, wherever
required, shall be guided by Chapter III of these Directions, unless specified
otherwise.
enriched_requirements.object: III of these Directions

conditions: If a resolution plan involving extension of original / extended DCCO
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If a resolution plan involving extension of original / extended DCCO, as the case
maybe, is implemented in a project finance account, which is classified as Standard
and satisfies all relevant prudential conditions specified for sanction, disbursement
and monitoring of project finance in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025, the asset classification of such
<image>
account shall continue to be classified as 'Standard', provided the envisaged
resolution plan ab initio conforms to the following conditions:
(1) Permitted DCCO Deferment - Original / extended DCCO, as the case may
be, is extended, along with the consequential shift in repayment schedule
for equal or shorter duration (including the start date and end date of revised
repayment schedule), within the following time limits:
Infrastructure
Non-Infrastructure Projects (including
Projects
CRE and CRE-RH) Permitted deferment of DCCO from the original DCCO
Up to 3 years Up to 2 years
Explanation: For CRE and CRE-RH projects, all provisions of the Real
Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, 2016 (as updated from time to
time) are to be complied with.
enriched_requirements.object: as 'Standard'

conditions:
where aggregate exposure of all lenders is less
than ₹100 crore
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Cost Overrun - An AIFI may finance, as part of a resolution plan, cost
overrun associated with permitted DCCO deferment in compliance with sub-
paragraph (1) above, and classify the account as 'Standard', as under:
Explanation: For projects where aggregate exposure of all lenders is less
than ₹100 crore, internal credit rating may be considered, if the project was
originally not credit rated externally.
enriched_requirements.object: a resolution plan, cost overrun associated with permitted DCCO deferment in compliance with sub- paragraph (1) above, and classify the account as 'Standard', as under: Explanation: For projects where aggregate exposure of all lenders is less than ₹100 crore, internal credit rating may be considered

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Change in Scope and Size - A project finance account where DCCO
extension is necessitated by an increase in the project outlay on account of
increase in scope and size of the project, may be classified as 'Standard',
subject to complying with the following conditions:

The standard asset classification benefit on account of 'change in scope' shall be
allowed only once during the lifetime of the project.
enriched_requirements.object: during the lifetime of the project

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In all the cases specified in paragraph 108, the following conditions shall be required
to be met in respect of all the lenders before the expiry of 180 days from the end of
the Review Period, for successful implementation of a resolution plan:
(1) all required documentation, including execution of necessary agreements
between a lender and the borrower / creation of security charge / perfection
of securities, are completed in consonance with the resolution plan being
implemented;
enriched_requirements.object: met in respect of all the lenders before the expiry of 180 days from the end of the Review Period, for successful implementation of

conditions:
If a resolution plan involving change in DCCO is not successfully implemented in
terms of paragraphs 108 and/ or 110
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If a resolution plan involving change in DCCO is not successfully implemented in
terms of paragraphs 108 and/ or 110, then the account shall be downgraded to NPA
immediately.
enriched_requirements.object: immediately

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: Satisfactory performance as defined in paragraph 4(14) shall apply in
this case.

A project finance account downgraded to NPA for non-compliance with paragraph
110, may be upgraded on successful implementation of resolution plan, provided no
further request for deferment of DCCO is received.
enriched_requirements.object: implementation of resolution plan

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Income recognition in respect of a project finance account where a resolution plans
involving extension of original / extended DCCO shall be as follows:
(1) An AIFI may recognise income on accrual basis in respect of project finance
exposures which are classified as 'Standard'.
enriched_requirements.object: An AIFI may recognise income on accrual basis in respect of project finance exposures which are classified as 'Standard'

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) For NPAs, income recognition shall be as per instructions contained in the
Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Income Recognition,
Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: contained in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisi

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For accounts which have availed DCCO deferment as per paragraphs 108 to 111
and are classified as 'standard', an AIFI shall maintain additional specific provisions
of 0.375 per cent for infrastructure project loans and 0.5625 per cent for non-
infrastructure project loans (including CRE and CRE-RH), for each quarter of
deferment, over and above the applicable standard asset provision specified in the
Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Income Recognition, Asset
Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: of 0

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The additional specific provisions required as per paragraph 115 shall be reversed
upon commencement of commercial operations.
enriched_requirements.object: of commercial operations

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions stipulated in paragraph 115 shall not be applicable for existing
projects which are specified in paragraph 99 and such project loans shall continue
to be guided by the prudential guidelines for the purpose of provisioning, prevailing
before October 1, 2025, shall apply, which otherwise shall be treated as repealed.
enriched_requirements.object: projects which are specified in paragraph 99 and such project loans shall continue to be guided by the prudential guideline

conditions: if these were sanctioned on or after October 1
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the instructions in paragraph 117, in case of any resolution of a
fresh credit event and / or change in material terms and conditions in the loan
contract in such projects, the provisions stipulated in paragraph 115 shall apply to
these projects as if these were sanctioned on or after October 1, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: as if these were sanctioned on or after October 1, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provisioning for project loans classified as 'standard' (construction or operational
phase) or NPA shall be as per extant instructions contained in the Reserve Bank of
India (All India Financial Institutions - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and
Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: instructions contained in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The instructions contained in this Chapter shall be applicable to resolution of
exposures of borrowers impacted by a natural calamity or, mutatis mutandis,
exposures of borrowers impacted by external events such as riots / disturbances
that result in loss to economic activity (hereinafter collectively referred to as
'calamity'), upon the declaration of such calamity by the Central/ State
Governments (in accordance with the framework placed by the concerned
Government for this purpose).
enriched_requirements.object: of exposures of borrowers impacted by a natural calamity or, mutatis mutandis, exposures of borrowers impacted by external

conditions:
where relief measures
have been already provided as on the effective date of these Amendment
Directions
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These instructions shall not apply to borrower accounts where relief measures
have been already provided as on the effective date of these Amendment
Directions, and such accounts shall continue to be guided by the existing
prudential guidelines.
enriched_requirements.object: where relief measures have been already provided as on the effective date of these Amendment Directions, and such accounts

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, any fresh resolution in such accounts subsequent
to the effective date of these Amendment Directions shall be as per the guidelines
contained in this Chapter.
enriched_requirements.object: guidelines contained in this Chapter

conditions:
if a larger part of the State / Union Territory (UT)
has been affected
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Upon declaration of a calamity, if a larger part of the State / Union Territory (UT)
has been affected, the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank shall convene a special
SLBC / UTLBC meeting within 15 days of such declaration.
enriched_requirements.object: / UTLBC meeting within 15 days of such declaration

conditions: If the calamity has affected only a part of the State / UT
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If the calamity has affected only a part of the State / UT, the convenor of the DCC
of the affected district(s) shall convene the meeting within 15 days of such
declaration, after due consultation with the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank.
enriched_requirements.object: 15 days of such declaration, after due consultation with the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In the special SLBC / UTLBC / DCC meeting, the position of the affected areas
may be assessed in terms of the severity of the impact of the calamity on the
economic activity and the need for any resolution measures by the REs.
enriched_requirements.object: of the severity of the impact of the calamity on the economic activity and the need for any resolution measures by the REs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
UTLBC / DCC may also formulate the objective criteria for identifying impacted
borrowers, and the extent of moratorium period, if any.
enriched_requirements.object: criteria for identifying impacted borrowers, and the extent of moratorium period

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The decisions taken in the special SLBC / UTLBC meeting(s) as mentioned at
paragraph 119E above, shall be conveyed by the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank
immediately to all SLBC / UTLBC members along with the minutes of the meeting.
enriched_requirements.object: SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank immediately to all SLBC / UTLBC members along with the minutes of the meeting

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A copy of the same shall also be forwarded by the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank
to the respective Regional Office of Reserve Bank and the Non-Banking Financial
Companies (NBFCs) / Urban Co-operative Banks (UCBs) operating in the area.
enriched_requirements.object: the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank to the respective Regional Office of Reserve Bank and the Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The decisions taken in the special DCC meeting(s) shall be conveyed by the DCC
convenor bank immediately to the DCC members and the SLBC / UTLBC along
with the minutes of the meeting.
enriched_requirements.object: DCC convenor bank immediately to the DCC members and the SLBC / UTLBC along with the minutes of the meeting

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A copy of the same shall also be forwarded by
the DCC convenor bank to the respective Regional Office of Reserve Bank and
the NBFCs / UCBs operating in the area.
enriched_requirements.object: the DCC convenor bank to the respective Regional Office of Reserve Bank and the NBFCs / UCBs operating in the area

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The decisions taken in the special SLBC / UTLBC / DCC meeting(s) shall be given
adequate publicity by SLBCs / UTLBC / DCCs / AIFI through various methods
such as brochures, banners, advertisement in newspapers, visits by field staff,
and other suitable modes, for the benefit of affected borrowers.
enriched_requirements.object: by SLBCs / UTLBC / DCCs / AIFI through various methods such as brochures, banners, advertisement in newspapers, visits by

conditions: Where the SLBC/ UTLBC/ DCC
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where the SLBC/ UTLBC/ DCC, as the case may be, recommends extending relief
measures to the impacted borrowers, AIFI may implement resolution plans in
respect of impacted borrowers subject to the provisions of this Chapter.
enriched_requirements.object: measures to the impacted borrowers, AIFI may implement resolution plans in respect of impacted borrowers subject to th

conditions:
In case no specific date of occurrence is ascertainable in respect of
a calamity
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: In case no specific date of occurrence is ascertainable in respect of
a calamity, the date of declaration of such calamity by the Central / State
Governments shall be treated as the date of occurrence.
enriched_requirements.object: date of occurrence

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Borrowers, whose loan accounts do not fulfil the required eligibility conditions for
resolution under this Chapter may continue to be considered for resolution under
other provisions of these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: for resolution under other provisions of these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The provisions of this Chapter shall not apply to the refinance portfolio of an AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: portfolio of an AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Those borrowers shall be eligible for resolution under this Chapter whose accounts
are classified as 'Standard', but which are not in default for more than 30 days
with the AIFI in respect of any of their facilities, as on the date of occurrence of
the calamity.
enriched_requirements.object: under this Chapter whose accounts are classified as 'Standard', but which are not in default for more than 30 days with the

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI may decide on participating in a particular DRS notified by a Government,
based on its Board approved policy, subject to the extant regulatory norms.
enriched_requirements.object: a particular DRS notified by a Government, based on its Board approved policy

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any provision of the scheme that may warrant modification in long term interest of
the borrowers or for prudential reasons may be duly brought to the notice of the
concerned authority/ies through the State Level Bankers' Committee / District level
Consultative Committee, during the consultation phase while designing the DRS.
enriched_requirements.object: term interest of the borrowers or for prudential reasons may be duly brought to the notice of the concerned authority/ies

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall clearly determine the eventual outstanding that may crystallise in their
books in respect of the borrowers proposed to be covered under the DRS, including
the accumulated interest in non-performing accounts, by the time the dues are
settled under the DRS, to enable the Government to suitably arrange for the extent
of fiscal participation.
enriched_requirements.object: outstanding that may crystallise in their books in respect of the borrowers proposed to be covered under the DRS, inclu

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall ensure that the borrowers to be covered under DRS are selected
strictly as per terms of such schemes so as to avoid subsequent non-admission by
the authorities on technical grounds.
enriched_requirements.object: to be covered under DRS are selected strictly as per terms of such schemes so as to avoid subsequent non-admission by the au

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The terms and conditions of the scheme as well as the prudential aspects, including
cooling period for extending fresh credit, impact on credit score etc., shall be clearly
communicated to the borrowers at the time of obtaining explicit consent from the
borrower for availing benefits under a proposed DRS.
enriched_requirements.object: the borrowers at the time of obtaining explicit consent from the borrower for availing benefits under a proposed DRS

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any waiver of accrued but unrealised interest and/ or sacrifice of principal
undertaken by an AIFI in the borrower accounts of beneficiaries of the DRS, either
as part of the implementation of the scheme or subsequent to its implementation,
shall be treated as a compromise settlement and shall attract the prudential
treatment contained in paragraphs 83 to 98.
enriched_requirements.object: compromise settlement and shall attract the prudential treatment contained in paragraphs 83 to 98

conditions:
If the funds received by an AIFI as part of the DRS covers the entire outstanding
dues of the borrower
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If the funds received by an AIFI as part of the DRS covers the entire outstanding
dues of the borrower, including principal and interest accrued till the date of receipt
of funds by the AIFI, the same shall lead to extinguishment of borrower's debt
obligations.
enriched_requirements.object: borrower's debt obligations

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where the funds received by an AIFI as part of the scheme are not adequate
to cover the entire outstanding dues of the borrower, leading to residual exposure
(principal and / or accrued interest), the asset classification of the residual exposure
shall be evaluated as per the terms and conditions of the original loan contract.
enriched_requirements.object: the terms and conditions of the original loan contract

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that any changes / modifications to the terms and conditions of the original
loan contract in such cases shall be evaluated against the test of restructuring as
defined in these Directions and shall attract the prudential treatment therein.
enriched_requirements.object: test of restructuring as defined in these Directions and shall attract the prudential treatment therein

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any fresh credit exposure to such borrowers shall be as per the commercial
discretion of the AIFI under relevant internal policy, subject to extant applicable
regulations.
enriched_requirements.object: commercial discretion of the AIFI under relevant internal policy

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI's reporting in respect of the borrowers under the scheme to the credit
information companies shall be guided by the extant guidelines in this regard.
enriched_requirements.object: extant guidelines in this regard

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
There shall not be creation of any receivable against the Government on account of
the DRS and the exposure shall continue to be on the borrower till receipt of funds
by the AIFI.
enriched_requirements.object: receivable against the Government on account of the DRS and the exposure shall continue to be on the borrower till receipt of funds

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Till receipt of funds, an AIFI shall continue to apply the prudential norms including
prudential norms on income recognition, asset classification and provisioning, and
wherever the accounts are non-performing, the AIFI may pursue recovery measures
as per their Board approved policy against such borrowers.
enriched_requirements.object: prudential norms including prudential norms on income recognition, asset classification and provisioning, and wherever the accou

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The instructions contained in paragraphs 120 to 131 shall apply in respect of DRS
notified on or after December 31, 2024 and shall be without prejudice to the
instructions on resolution of stressed assets contained in these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: DRS notified on or after December 31, 2024 and shall be without prejudice to the instructions on resolution of stressed assets con

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of relief measures announced prior to December 31, 2024, any dues
pending receipt from Government, for more than 90 days shall attract specific
provision of 100 per cent.
enriched_requirements.object: 100 per cent

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall take necessary action and actively follow up with the respective
Governments for settlement of dues referred to in paragraph 134.
enriched_requirements.object: actively follow up with the respective Governments for settlement of dues referred to in paragraph 134

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Paragraphs 136 to 138 shall be applicable to cases where an act of restructuring
creates new securities issued by the borrower which would be held by an AIFI in
lieu of a portion of the pre-restructured exposure.
enriched_requirements.object: where an act of restructuring creates new securities issued by the borrower which would be held by an AIFI in lieu of a portion

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Funded Interest Term Loan (FITL) / debt / equity instruments created by
conversion of principal / unpaid interest, as the case may be, shall be placed in the
same asset classification category by an AIFI in which the restructured advance has
been classified.
enriched_requirements.object: same asset classification category by an AIFI in which the restructured advance has been classified

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The provisioning applicable to instruments acquired as part of restructuring shall be
the higher of:
(1) The provisioning applicable to the asset classification category in which
such instruments are held by an AIFI;
enriched_requirements.object: (1) The provisioning applicable to the asset classification category in which such instruments are held by an AIFI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The overarching principle should be that valuation of instruments arising out of
resolution of stressed assets shall be based on conservative assessment of cash
flows and appropriate discount rates to reflect the stressed cash flows of the
borrowers.
enriched_requirements.object: assessment of cash flows and appropriate discount rates to reflect the stressed cash flows of the borrowers

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Statutory Auditors should specifically examine as to whether the valuations of such
instruments reflect the risk of loss associated with such instruments.
enriched_requirements.object: whether the valuations of such instruments reflect the risk of loss associated with such instruments

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Debt / quasi-debt / equity instruments shall be subject to all the instructions
contained in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions -
Classification, Valuation and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025 to
the extent they are not inconsistent with these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: the instructions contained in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Classification, Valuation and Operation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Debentures / bonds shall be valued as per the instructions contained in the Reserve
Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Classification, Valuation and
Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: the instructions contained in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Classification, Valuation and Operation o

conditions:
Where the borrower fails to build up the sinking fund as required under the
above Directions
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
part of a resolution plan and the valuation of such instruments shall be as prescribed
in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Classification,
Valuation and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025, subject to the
following:
(1) Where the borrower fails to build up the sinking fund as required under the
above Directions, ZCBs / LCBs of such borrower shall be collectively valued
at ₹1;
enriched_requirements.object: the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Classification, Valuation and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025

conditions: where classified as standard
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Equity instruments, where classified as standard, shall be valued at market value, if
quoted, or else, should be valued at the lowest value arrived using the following
valuation methodologies:
(1) Book value (without considering 'revaluation reserves', if any) which is to be
ascertained from the company's latest audited balance sheet.
enriched_requirements.object: value

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The date as
on which the latest balance sheet is drawn up should not precede the date
of valuation by more than 18 months.
enriched_requirements.object: valuation by more than 18 months

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, cash flows (cash flow available from the
current as well as immediately prospective (not more than six months) level
of operations) occurring within 85 per cent of the useful economic life of the
project only shall be reckoned.

conditions: where classified as NPA
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Equity instruments, where classified as NPA, shall be valued at market value, if
quoted, or else, shall be collectively valued at ₹1.
enriched_requirements.object: value

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Preference Shares shall be valued as per the instructions contained in the Reserve
Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Classification, Valuation and
Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025, subject to the following
modifications:
(1) The discount rate shall be subject to a floor of weighted average actual
interest rate charged to the borrower on the residual debt after restructuring
plus a mark-up of 1.5 percent.
enriched_requirements.object: the instructions contained in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Classification, Valuation and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025

conditions: Where preference dividends / coupons are in arrears

if arrears are for one
year
if arrears are for two years
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Where preference dividends / coupons are in arrears, no credit should be
taken for accrued dividends / coupons and the value determined as above
should be discounted further by at least 15 per cent if arrears are for one
year, 25 per cent if arrears are for two years, so on and so forth (i.e., with
annual increments of ten per cent).
enriched_requirements.object: dividends / coupons and the value determined as above should be discounted further by at least 15 per cent if arrears are for one

conditions:
In case an AIFI has acquired unquoted instruments on conversion of debt as a part
of a resolution plan
if the resolution plan is not deemed as implemented
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case an AIFI has acquired unquoted instruments on conversion of debt as a part
of a resolution plan, and if the resolution plan is not deemed as implemented, such
unquoted instruments shall collectively be valued at ₹1 at that point, and till the
resolution plan is treated as implemented.
enriched_requirements.object: ₹1 at that point, and till the resolution plan is treated as implemented

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The unrealised income represented by FITL / Debt or equity instrument should have
a corresponding credit in an account styled as 'Sundry Liabilities Account (Interest
Capitalization)'.
enriched_requirements.object: in an account styled as 'Sundry Liabilities Account (Interest Capitalization)'

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Without prejudice to the requirements of Section 19 (2) of the Banking Regulation
Act, 1949, acquisition of shares due to conversion of debt to equity during a
restructuring process shall be exempted from regulatory ceilings / restrictions on
Capital Market Exposures, investment in Para-Banking activities and intra-group
exposure.
enriched_requirements.object: ceilings / restrictions on Capital Market Exposures, investment in Para-Banking activities and intra-group exposure

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding paragraph 152, such acquisition shall require reporting to
Department of Supervision, Reserve Bank of India, CO every month along with the
regular DBS Return on Asset Quality and disclosure by the AIFI in the Notes to
Accounts in Annual Financial Statements.
enriched_requirements.object: of Supervision, Reserve Bank of India, CO every month along with the regular DBS Return on Asset Quality and disclosur

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
With reference to the requirements contained in sub-regulations 158 (6) (a) of ICDR
Regulations, 2018, the issue price of the equity shall be the lower of (1) or (2) below:
(1) The average of the weekly high and low of the volume weighted average
price of the related equity shares quoted on the recognised stock exchange
during the 26 weeks preceding the 'reference date' or the average of the
weekly high and low of the volume weighted average prices of the related
<image>
equity shares quoted on a recognised stock exchange during the two weeks
preceding the 'reference date', whichever is lower;
enriched_requirements.object: (1) or (2) below: (1) The average of the weekly high and low of the volume weighted average price of the related equity shares quoted

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The date
as on which the latest balance sheet is drawn up should not precede the
date of restructuring by more than 18 months.
enriched_requirements.object: restructuring by more than 18 months

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of conversion of debt into equity, the 'reference date' shall be the date
on which the AIFI approves the restructuring scheme.
enriched_requirements.object: which the AIFI approves the restructuring scheme

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of conversion of
convertible securities into equity, the 'reference date' shall be the date on which the
AIFI approves the conversion of the convertible securities into equities.
enriched_requirements.object: which the AIFI approves the conversion of the convertible securities into equities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
To mitigate the burden of debt servicing brought about by trade disruptions caused
by global headwinds and to ensure the continuity of viable businesses, AIFIs may
extend relief measures to eligible borrowers, as specified under Reserve Bank of
India (Trade Relief Measures) Directions, 2025 dated November 14, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: eligible borrowers, as specified under Reserve Bank of India (Trade Relief Measures) Directions, 2025 dated November 14, 202

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The directions,
instructions and guidelines already repealed vide any of the directions, instructions,
and guidelines listed in the above notification shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof.
enriched_requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

conditions: if those directions
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
enriched_requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being
in force.
enriched_requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

conditions: if it considers necessary
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the Reserve Bank may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any
provision of these Directions given by the Reserve Bank shall be final and binding.

For the purpose of the Model Operating Procedure (MOP), Debt Relief Schemes (DRS)
refer to Schemes notified by the State Governments that entail funding by the fiscal
authorities to cover debt obligations of a targeted segment of borrowers that the regulated
entities are required to sacrifice / waive.

Announcement / notification of any such DRS should include the specific stress or
distress situation necessitating announcement of such support.
enriched_requirements.object: or distress situation necessitating announcement of such support

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Given the broader
implications of such DRS for the credit culture, while broad based relief measures can be
addressed through pure fiscal support in the form of Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT), DRS
should be considered only as a measure of last resort when other measures to alleviate
financial stress have failed.
enriched_requirements.object: a measure of last resort when other measures to alleviate financial stress have failed

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Before announcing any DRS, Governments may engage with the State Level Bankers'
Committee (SLBC)/ District level Consultative Committee (DCC) to evolve a coordinated
action plan for conceptualisation, design, and implementation of the DRS.
enriched_requirements.object: Level Bankers' Committee (SLBC)/ District level Consultative Committee (DCC) to evolve a coordinated action plan for conceptuali

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The schemes
should, cover critical aspects of the scheme like identification of borrowers, impact
assessment, implementation timelines, resolution of issues concerning settlement of dues
by Government to the lending institutions, etc.
enriched_requirements.object: of the scheme like identification of borrowers, impact assessment, implementation timelines, resolution of issues concerning

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The design features should ensure that the DRS do not impact the financial stability
aspects of the region / State or create moral hazards in the borrower segments.
enriched_requirements.object: do not impact the financial stability aspects of the region / State or create moral hazards in the borrower segments

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: should also be taken into account.
enriched_requirements.object: account

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Detailed budgetary provisions / funding may be provided upfront towards any proposed
DRS to fully cover the required settlement amounts.
enriched_requirements.object: any proposed DRS to fully cover the required settlement amounts

conditions:
Where regulated entities have dues
<image>
from the Government
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where regulated entities have dues
<image>
from the Government, pertaining to earlier DRS schemes, new schemes should be
announced only on a fully pre-funded basis.
enriched_requirements.object: a fully pre-funded basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The DRS should be targeted only at the impacted borrowers and should not contain
any restrictive covenant against timely repayments.
enriched_requirements.object: the impacted borrowers and should not contain any restrictive covenant against timely repayments

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, it should specify the criteria
for determining eligible borrowers on an objective basis, detailed timeline of critical /
material events, including cut-off dates for filing/ submission, acknowledgement, approval
and settlement of claims along with compensation clauses for delays in settling the funds,
on part of the Government.
enriched_requirements.object: determining eligible borrowers on an objective basis, detailed timeline of critical / material events, including cut-off date

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The DRS should cover the entire outstanding dues of the borrowers being covered,
including principal and accumulated interest till the date of receipt of funds by the
regulated entities from the Government.
enriched_requirements.object: dues of the borrowers being covered, including principal and accumulated interest till the date of receipt of funds by th

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The DRS should not require the creation of a receivable in the books of the regulated
entity against the Government.
enriched_requirements.object: a receivable in the books of the regulated entity against the Government

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The exposure of regulated entities to the borrower shall
continue and shall be reduced to the extent of funds received from the Government.
enriched_requirements.object: reduced to the extent of funds received from the Government

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The entire implementation of the Scheme and settlement of claims by the Governments
to the regulated entities, should generally be completed within 45 to 60 days.
enriched_requirements.object: 45 to 60 days

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The DRS should not contain any provision contrary to any regulatory instruction issued
by RBI / NABARD.
enriched_requirements.object: to any regulatory instruction issued by RBI / NABARD

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The design of the DRS should not contain any provision that casts any obligations on
the regulated entities, directly or indirectly, to:
a.
enriched_requirements.object: casts any obligations on the regulated entities, directly or indirectly, to: a

conditions:
if the regulated entities agree to any of the above at the time of design of DRS
or subsequently
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
However, if the regulated entities agree to any of the above at the time of design of DRS
or subsequently, as per their Board-approved policies, it shall be subject to the applicable
prudential guidelines.
enriched_requirements.object: applicable prudential guidelines
